name: Rugelach
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the margarine with cream cheese in a large bowl using an electric mixer.
2. Gradually add flour and blend well.
3. Divide the dough into four parts and chill wrapped in waxed paper for 3-4 hours.
4. To shape: On a floured surface, roll out one portion of dough and cut into circles with a round cookie cutter. You may need to gently knead in a small amount of additional flour.
5. Place half a teaspoon of raspberry filling mixture in the center of each circle. Bring the sides towards the center or fold in half to form a crescent shape.
6. Roll out the next portion of dough into a rectangle and spread the chocolate filling mixture over it.
7. Roll out another portion of dough into a matching rectangle and cover with the chocolate rectangle.
8. Cut into small squares using the remaining dough.
9. Place teaspoonfuls of apricot filling mixture in the center of each square. Top with half of a dried apricot.
10. Bring the sides of dough towards the center to form triangles. Optionally, roll the other half of dough into a large circle and cut into triangle shapes.
11. Spread the apricot, preserve, and nut mixture on the triangles.
12. Bake in an oven preheated to 350 degrees for 12-13 minutes.
13. Cool slightly and then roll in confectioner's sugar before serving.
14. Store in airtight containers. Makes approximately 40 cookies. Keep in airtight containers in a cool place.
